name: sepsis_therapy_requires_cultures_with_antimicrobials
expected_issue_substrings: infection time-critical actions must include cultures

key_teaching_points: Septic shock requires early recognition and immediate escalation
Antibiotics within 1 hour and fluid resuscitation are time-critical
Lactate elevation suggests tissue hypoperfusion
clinical_red_flags: Hypotension with fever and altered mental status
Lactate 4.1 mmol/L suggesting tissue hypoperfusion
AKI and poor perfusion suggesting organ dysfunction
time_critical_actions: Start broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour
Begin sepsis fluid resuscitation and reassessment
contraindication_checks: Renal impairment and allergy history before antibiotic selection or dosing
Volume overload risk during fluid resuscitation in CKD or heart failure
Need for vasopressors if hypotension persists after initial resuscitation

name: dka_requires_potassium_and_closure_actions
expected_issue_substrings: DKA time-critical actions must include potassium-before-insulin

key_teaching_points: DKA criteria include acidosis and ketones
Insulin and fluids are time-critical after initial safety assessment
Anion gap closure is the transition endpoint
clinical_red_flags: Severe metabolic acidosis with Kussmaul respirations
Tachycardia, dehydration signs, AKI, and mild confusion
Hyperkalemia despite total body potassium depletion
time_critical_actions: Start insulin infusion after DKA is recognized
Begin fluid resuscitation for dehydration
Identify precipitating cause
contraindication_checks: Potassium below safe threshold before insulin infusion
Cerebral edema risk from overly rapid osmolar shifts
Need to exclude surgical abdomen if pain persists after metabolic correction

name: stroke_requires_last_known_normal_and_imaging_actions
expected_issue_substrings: stroke time-critical actions must include last-known-normal timing

key_teaching_points: Time-sensitive stroke reperfusion eligibility depends on last known normal
Noncontrast head CT is required before thrombolysis decisions
NIHSS and large vessel occlusion screening guide thrombectomy planning
clinical_red_flags: Sudden focal neurologic deficit with NIHSS 8
Potentially treatable stroke within thrombolysis window
Atrial fibrillation with missed anticoagulation suggesting embolic risk
time_critical_actions: Activate stroke pathway immediately
Obtain noncontrast head CT to exclude hemorrhage without delaying treatment decision
Assess thrombolysis and thrombectomy eligibility in parallel
contraindication_checks: Intracranial hemorrhage or early extensive ischemic change on imaging
Recent anticoagulant use, bleeding history, platelet count, glucose, and blood pressure thresholds
Large vessel occlusion criteria and transfer needs for thrombectomy

name: pe_requires_risk_and_imaging_actions
expected_issue_substrings: PE time-critical actions must include risk stratification

overrides.diagnosis: Pulmonary Embolism with right ventricular strain
key_teaching_points: Wells criteria and clinical probability guide PE diagnostic strategy
RV strain and hemodynamic instability determine PE risk category
CTPA or bedside echo pathway depends on hemodynamic stability
clinical_red_flags: Sudden dyspnea with hypoxemia and pleuritic chest pain after surgery
Tachycardia with borderline blood pressure and signs of right heart strain
Unilateral calf swelling and markedly elevated D-dimer
time_critical_actions: Start anticoagulation planning for suspected pulmonary embolism
Give supplemental oxygen and monitor closely
Escalate if symptoms worsen
contraindication_checks: Bleeding risk, recent surgery, and neuraxial anesthesia before thrombolysis or anticoagulation
Renal function and contrast allergy before CT pulmonary angiography
Pregnancy status when selecting imaging and anticoagulation
